content.problemStatement:
Chef has a directed graph consisting of nnn nodes, numbered from 000 to n−1n-1n−1. Each node may have edges directing to other nodes, forming paths through the graph. 

Chef wants to find all the nodes from which every possible path eventually leads to a terminal node — a node with no outgoing edges — or another safe node. Such nodes are called "safe nodes" because starting from them, Chef will never get stuck in a cycle.

Help Chef by identifying all these safe nodes in the graph. Chef needs the list of safe nodes sorted in ascending order so that he can analyze his graph efficiently.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindSafeNodesfindSafeNodesfindSafeNodes - This function identifies all nodes in a directed graph that are eventually safe, meaning no path starting from these nodes leads to a cycle.

Parameters
directedGraphdirectedGraphdirectedGraph : A list where each element represents a node and contains an array of integers indicating the nodes it directs to.
The graph is represented as an adjacency list with node indices ranging from 000 to graphNodeCount−1graphNodeCount - 1graphNodeCount−1.
Return Value
Returns an array containing all safe nodes in ascending order.
Each element represents a node index that is eventually safe.
The output list is sorted in ascending order.
Constraints
1≤n≤1041 \leq n \leq 10^41≤n≤104
0≤directedGraph[i].size()≤n0 \leq \text{directedGraph}[i].\text{size}() \leq n0≤directedGraph[i].size()≤n
0≤directedGraph[i][j]≤n−10 \leq \text{directedGraph}[i][j] \leq n - 10≤directedGraph[i][j]≤n−1
Each `directedGraph[i]` is sorted in strictly increasing order.
The graph may contain self-loops.
The total number of edges in the graph is in the range [1,4×104][1, 4 \times 10^4][1,4×104].

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT — number of test cases:
For each test case:
The first line contains an integer nnn — number of nodes.
The next nnn lines describe the graph:
Each line contains the adjacency list of node i in the format: 

[u1 v1] 

[u2 v2] 

...


Output Format
Print all safe nodes of the first test case only, in ascending order, separated by spaces.

solution.timeComplexity: O(V + E), since each node and edge is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(V + E), for storing the reversed graph and indegree counts.
